title: Planar depth representations for block compression
abstract: Described herein are technologies related to facilitate high precision and resolution of a depth (Z) buffer storage during a process of rendering 3D scenes. More particularly, during an interpolation, encoding, and/or storing processes in a graphic pipeline for rendering the 3D scenes, a particular depth (Z) plane representation is configured to support an un-normalized depth and a floating point depth formats that may be used to store Z values to the Z buffer storage.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A graphics processing unit (GPU), the GPU comprising: one or more graphics cores configured to process computer graphics, the one or more graphics cores being further configured to operate with a memory; the one or more graphics cores being further configured to: generate a Z-plane for a triangle that covers at least one multi- sample pixel which has multiple samples; produce a reformatted Z-plane configured to store in a memory in a block compression format, the format includes a reference depth field configured to store a reference depth value, a slope field configured to store two slope values, and a slope shift field that specifies a right shift for the two slope values; process the reformatted Z-plane to produce depth values of one or more of the samples of the multiple samples in the pixels covered by the block compression format. 2. A GPU recited in claim 1, wherein the depth values produced are unormalized integers representing depth values in a range of zero to one. 3. A GPU as recited in claim 1, wherein the reference depth field is configured to store a 47-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 5- bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 26-bit slope values. 4. A GPU recited in claim 1, wherein, the reference depth field is configured to store a 35-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 4- bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 19-bit slope values. 5. A GPU recited in claim 1, wherein the format further includes an exponent field and the depth values produced are floating point values. 6. A GPU as recited in claim 5, wherein the exponent field is configured to store an 8-bit value, the reference depth field is configured to store a 47-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 5-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 26-bit slope values. 7. A GPU recited in claim 5, wherein the exponent field is configured to store an 8-bit value, the reference depth field is configured to store a 35-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 4-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 19-bit slope values. 8. One or more computer-readable media storing instructions thereon that, when executed by one or more processors, direct the one or more processors to perform operations comprising: generating a Z-plane for a triangle that covers at least one multi-sample pixel which has multiple samples; producing a reformatted Z-plane configured to store in a memory in a block compression format, the format includes a reference depth field configured to store a reference depth value, a slope field configured to store two slope values, and a slope shift field that specifies a right shift for the two slope values; processing the reformatted Z-plane to produce depth values of one or more of the samples of the multiple samples in the pixels covered by the block compression format. 9. One or more computer-readable media of claim 8, wherein the depth values produced are unormalized integers representing depth values in a range of zero to one. 10. One or more computer-readable media of claim 8, wherein the reference depth field is configured to store a 47-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 5-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 26-bit slope values. 11. One or more computer-readable media of claim 8, wherein, the reference depth field is configured to store a 35-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 4-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 19-bit slope values. 12. One or more computer-readable media of claim 8, wherein the format further includes an exponent field and the depth values produced are floating point values. 13. One or more computer-readable media of claim 12, wherein the exponent field is configured to store an 8-bit value, the reference depth field is configured to store a 47-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 5-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 26-bit slope values. 14. One or more computer-readable media of claim 12, wherein the exponent field is configured to store an 8-bit value, the reference depth field is configured to store a 35-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 4-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 19-bit slope values. 15. A computer graphics system comprising: a Z-plane generator configured to generate a Z-plane for a triangle that covers at least one multi-sample pixel which has multiple samples; 

 a Z-plane reformatter configured to produce a reformatted Z-plane configured to store in a memory in a block compression format, the format includes a reference depth field configured to store a reference depth value, a slope field configured to store two slope values, and a slope shift field that specifies a right shift for the two slope values; a Z-plane processor configured to process the reformatted Z-plane to produce depth values of one or more of the samples of the multiple samples in the pixels covered by the block compression format. 16. A system of claim 15, wherein the depth values produced are unormalized integers representing depth values in a range of zero to one. 17. A system of claim 15, wherein the reference depth field is configured to store a 47-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 5- bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 26-bit slope values. 18. A system of claim 15, wherein, the reference depth field is configured to store a 35-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 4- bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 19-bit slope values. 19. A system of claim 15, wherein the format further includes an exponent field and the depth values produced are floating point values. 20. A system of claim 19, wherein the exponent field is configured to store an 8-bit value, the reference depth field is configured to store a 47-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 5-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 26-bit slope values. 21. A system of claim 19, wherein the exponent field is configured to store an 8-bit value, the reference depth field is configured to store a 35-bit value, the slope shift field configured to store a 4-bit value, and the slope field configured to store two 19-bit slope values.
